Description: You've entered the Depot, the market area for the Crimson Fleet. Weapons, armor, general goods, neuroamps; everything you need is in one place. The space itself is a narrow corridor, shops flanking you on both sides. The area feels pinched together, with the stores almost right on top of you as the shopkeeps goad you for some business. Whether it's out of necessity (it used to be a UC station with no market) or by design (the Fleet chose this location for the Depot) is unclear.

Player is leaving a person in need stranded in the middle of nowhere with a malfunctioning ship. it's not a death sentence, but certainly doesn't speak well of the player's character. Like ignoring someone on the side of the road with a flat tire in the middle of a desert.

Sasha has just explained that she was confronting someone with whom she's had a checkered past, and he and his friends attacked and beat her badly enough to require a legnthy medical stay.Opinions on standing up for oneself regardless of the odds, or picking a fight when you're outnumbered, are welcome.

The top level, still deep underground, of something that might be an underground storage facility or archives. They need to find their way down deeper into the facility. There are various ways to get down and find they're way to the artifact. The artifact isn't far away.
This room contains the artifact in a large device. Here NASA Scientists long ago studied the artifact and worked on what could be described as a prototype grav drive.

Outside, on the surface of Bindi, near what looks to be a collapsed mine or cave, is the skeletal remains of the last sister, with an odd assortment of items. Comment should be something about 'guessing that____ was important?!?'
Moving upstairs, towards the living quarters, nothing specific, but having the compantion touch on something about 'they weren't much for house keeping' referrring to how filthy the place is.
About 1/3 of the way into the interior, walking through connecting space, nothing in particular of interest - more being curious as to what happend and where did the sisters go.

Player says 'you need to be strong now' when returning an audio log of a widow's dying husband. (shows toughness and grit, could also be read as a little callous... but also just a 'keep a stiff upper lip' kind of advice)
Player says 'It sounds like he loved you and your son very much' when returning an audio log of a widow's dying husband. (shows empathy)
Player says 'sorry for your loss' when returning an audio log of a widow's dying husband. (shows sympathy)

There is an Helium 3 extractor under construction.

Description: This is the main entrance of the base. It's heavily reinforced. The gate door has been forced and is wide open.
There is a long bridge in front of you. It's made of concrete and metal. Parts of it are still under construction

You enter the base courtyard under construction. It's build inside the mountain (like Cheyenne mountain base in stargate). The structure is mostly made of concrete and metal. There is a control tower in the center of the courtyard.
The is a large window in the lobby from where you can see an engineering section.
You enter a control hallway with a gate and a scanner. Through the window you can see a reactor.
It's a large cave room where it looks like the digging activities has been stop. There is an elevator in the other side of the room in a tunnel.

This is a huge cave hall. In the far back you can see the buried Starborn temple with rocks spinning around it. The center of the Hall is filled with tall rock formations with arc formation (Starborn technology) on top of it.
You're inside the buried Starborn temple. Parts of it collapse and part of it has been pierced by long Crystal.

The Player is speaking to a Trade Authority representative in Cydonia. They're trying to sell an Aurora shipment (that was given to them by UC SysDef as a 'buy in') to get into the Crimson Fleet. Player is using all the leverage they can to convince her to point them the right way. In this case, the Player was clever and talked her into it using Industrialist skill. Very clever.

The Player is speaking to a Trade Authority representative in Cydonia. They're trying to sell an Aurora shipment (that was given to them by UC SysDef as a 'buy in') to get into the Crimson Fleet. Player is using all the leverage they can to convince her to point them the right way. Player resorts to a bribe.

'In blunt terms: you have compromising materials about me in your possession. The only thing I have to offer in return are my GalBank credentials.' Dumbrosky has intuited why the Player is there, and as usual, he's trying to slime his way out of it. He's calm and collected even though he's been 'caught' in his conspiracy to defraud GalBank.

A lone homestead, with a greenhouse, a water cistern, a windmill. There are some stone cairns arranged in circles or perhaps orbits. There is a small home in the typical settlement style. It has been abandoned for a long time. The plants in the greenhouse, combined with a leaking water cistern have created a small oasis in the area.
in a secluded area, a small island or plateau some scorpion like creatures are visible and surrounded by large Starborn ruins. There is a large scorpion statue at the center of these Starborn ruins.
Soldier says you can pass, just be quick about it. That goes for your friend too. Companion then responds.
The player has notified the Ragana that the Fleet want one of their crew members dead. They state they are open to negotiation and would like to discuss a peaceful solution.
Player healed a wounded person, saving them from an almost certain death.
Player healed a wounded actor. It is assumed that this person is deserving of receiving medical treatment. (This event will fire whenever the player heals an actor using the SQ_Wounded system)
The companion sees the facility with creatures having taken over and creature nests in the main entrance of the housing section.
Really big hole in the ground into a large creature nest/tunnel. Is in the middle of the lowest level of the living area.
Another large hole the player drops down, this is a large tank for liquids in an industrial/medical lab setting (clones in the origin quest line).
First big view of the underground lab/factory after coming out of the big water tank.
Narrow and dimly lit corridor in the mechanical/storage section of a ship. The hallway is lined with robot pods.
The top of a long set of stairs down a concrete tunnel, like the interior of a dam. Its inside a 'superhero lair' (for the Mantis quest) leading to a cave.
There is a cliffside mining/oil rig. Connecting to it is a new section under construction and a small construction site.
Break room in between control center and garages in an ecliptic compound (industrial outpost base). Light hearted/satirical comment on the break room.
First view from the landing pad of a large Ecliptic base. There is a bridge with containers and outposts built along the cliff.
View of an Ecliptic bar/lounge and central outpost building. The room is kind of neon themed and is a large two story hex with a central circular bar.
Exterior view of a large science base that is overgrown and in disarray. this is near the main entrance.
First view of the interior being totally overgrown.
View of large central atrium, with small pond and several floors with balconies overlooking it. There are large creatures moving around the pond in this room too.
View of a ship being assembled and worked on out on a  large tarmac with a big construction rig next to it. on both sides of the panoramic are large warehouses.
Inside one of the large warehouses on the second floor overlooking a crane with a large chunk of a ship lifted into the air. towards the left you can see the central ship that is being worked on.
In the middle of the lab/greenhouse at the back of the building.
Player just rescued Andreja and was kind.

Player kills Rusty, the man responsible for the death of Katherine Luzion's husband. The death was an accident, but Rusty is still responsible, as he was trying to force the husband to pay him credits. (there's no arrest option so it's death or not)

The player chooses to spare Rusty, the man who is responsible for the death of Katherine Luzion's husband. The death was an accident, but Rusty is still responsible, as he was trying to force the husband to pay him credits. (there's no arrest option so it's death or not)
Confronting a smuggler baron who tries to make a make a deal with (IE bribe) the player, the player chooses the attack prompt and begins combat

The Player is speaking to a Trade Authority representative in Cydonia. They're trying to sell an Aurora shipment (that was given to them by UC SysDef as a 'buy in') to get into the Crimson Fleet. Player is using all the leverage they can to convince her to point them the right way. In this case, the Player threatens her with violence.

Confronting a smuggler baron who tries to make a make a deal with the player the player uses their bargaining skill to make a counter offer, agreeing to let the smuggler baron go if they stay out of Freestar space.

The Player is dealing with Adler Kemp, the person that can get them into the Fleet. He said he'll help, but in typical CF fashion, he wants a favor first (taking advantage). This is the only way Player can get into the CF. The favor is to get an owed debt from a drunk. The Player agrees to help in different ways. In this version, the Player mentions his Bouncer past and how he can deal with drunks.

The Player is dealing with Adler Kemp, the person that can get them into the Fleet. He said he'll help, but in typical CF fashion, he wants a favor first (taking advantage). This is the only way Player can get into the CF. The favor is to get an owed debt from a drunk. The Player agrees to help in different ways. In this version, the Player offers to pay off the drunks debt at that moment (before even confronting him).

The player lies to Nadia, telling her that House Varuun will attack and hurt people if Nadia doesn't upload a radio broadcast to SSNN. This is an illegal action, and it bypasses having the player do it themselves.

The player tells Nadia Muffaz the truth about what's happening with a tree, and gets her to broadcast the radio broadcast that will save the tree. This bypasses having the player do it themselves, which is technically illegal.
The player chooses to buy an ancient tree branch from Earl Fulton for 500 dollars instead of stealing it from Leah Casler.
Ikande thanked the player from bringing back the Legacy. The player stated they just want to do the right thing.
Leah Casler says that she doesn't like the UC, and that she's reluctant to help the UC with anything. Even if it's just handing over a tree branch.
Kelton tells the player to upload a file to the SSNN kiosks. If the player asks if that's illegal, Kelton will candidly say yes, but it's a necessary desperate measure.

After a successful speech challenge, the player tells a shady character to hand over an important data slate. Normally, if the player failed this speech challenge, they'd have to do something illegal for it.
If the player wins the speech challenge, Emilio will offer the player more money to do something illegal for them, and he seems a bit desperate.

The player agrees to do something shady and illegal for a man named Emilio in order to get a data slate from them. The player might not know for sure what they're supposed to do just yet, but they know it's shady.

If the player tells Kelton they're working on retrieving his bio sensors, Kelton tells off the player for not having all of the bio sensors in their possession yet. He proceeds to dramatically say that getting the bio sensors is critical, time-sensitive, and critical. All of this is over a tree.

Naeva Mora just told the Player if she doesn't get the special item she's sending the Player to Siren of the Stars to get, she'll basically take it out of their regular pay - so it's Optional but it's really not (forcing Player to do it).
Affinity event for when the player purchases the bio sensor from Wen Tseng, instead of stealing it.
Affinity event for when the player steals a bio sensor from the UC Distribution Center.
Player just rescued Andreja but was stern.
Player just rescued Barrett and was kind
Player just rescued Barrett but was stern

The Player is dealing with Adler Kemp, the person that can get them into the Fleet. He said he'll help, but in typical CF fashion, he wants a favor first (taking advantage). This is the only way Player can get into the CF. The favor is to get an owed debt from a drunk. The Player says he'll just kill the drunk and take the money off his corpse. Harsh!
Player says 'Given it was a UC station, I did my best not to hurt anyone.' Kibwe says 'And on behalf of the United Colonies and Commander Natara, I thank you.'
Player says it was tricky going through that whole station without getting caught. Kibwe says 'But you did it with grace and aplomb. You'd make a fine spy, if we were still at war.'
Player says 'I'd never take the lives of my fellow soldiers.' Kibwe says Honor, loyalty, and valor are attributes we look for in a SysDef soldier. Undercover or not, you have them.'
Player has just agreed to Huan Daiyu's conditions that they serve as a member of her crew, but the risk is entirely the player's and their companion's. Huan will bail at the first sign of trouble.

Player exits the airlock and sees the vista of the massive, frozen crater that the Red Mile endurance course takes place in. In the distance is the tower where the checkpoint that the contestants have to reach as part of the course. Most people who run this course do not make it back alive.
You are approaching Genghis Khan. You know he's a clone. At this point you can see he is in Mongolian attire. It's a surreal experience.

LC102 is the 'Siren of the Stars' luxury cruise ship. In this event, the companion has just entered one of the crew sleeping areas. There are four beds in a communal, dorm-style room. LC102 is the 'Siren of the Stars' luxury cruise ship. In this event, the companion has just followed the player into the life-support maintenance room. LC102 is the 'Siren of the Stars' luxury cruise ship. In this event, the companion has just approached one of the giant windows in the ship's lavish ballroom. It's quite a view to see, from inside a ship.

LC102 is the 'Siren of the Stars' luxury cruise ship. All the guests who were previously in the ballroom have evacuated back to their suites. The ballroom is now oddly empty. The companion might comment about how the ballroom it feels different without any people in it, how much larger it feels, quieter (though there will be alarm siren sounds in the background), etc. This is meant to be a passing comment, to accentuate the change in atmosphere.

The player has just arrived on the ECS Constant, an ancient colony ship. Neither the player, nor the colonists knew what to expect when the player boarded. When greeted at the entrance, the ship's captain seems surprised that the player is human, since they didn't expect to find anyone out here (the ECS Constant left before Earth's collapse and assumed they'd be the first and/or only humans to be here when they arrived) The player responds 'Can't you see? I'm actually a little green monster with tentacles.'

Addressing Captain Diana Brackenridge. The player has successfully negotiated with Captain Brackenridge to allow Janet Yang to leave the ECS Constant, even though she insists that none of the other colonists leave the ship. Brackenridge is young and inexperienced, so letting her know she made the right choice here might help her.

Addressing Captain Diana Brackenridge. The player just traded a sack of potatoes to Captain Brackenridge to make up for the temporary loss of food production that the ECS Constant would face by letting Janet Yang go off by herself. Janet is now free to go. This was a generous, though strange deal. There's also the issue that the fragility of the ship's ecosystem: if letting one person go would impact them so much.

Addressing Oliver Campbell. The player has successfully negotiated with Oliver to release Janet Yang from her debt to Paradiso. The player made the case that this would be better for everyone involved, because Janet is so unhappy, she may actually be a liability. Oliver has agreed on the condition that the player not tell any other of the ECS Constant colonists about this one-time deal.

Addressing the player. The player just donated a bunch of credits to pay off Janet Yang's debt to Paradiso, a very generous action. Then again, Janet has had a pretty sour attitude up until now, so maybe this is unwarranted.

Addressing Jiro. The player has returned to Jiro after helping him with a belligerent hotel guest. In this case, the player has subdued the guest through violence. There are two potential paths that led the player here. In this case, the guest attacked first. The companion might suggest that there was no other way, and might be impressed with how the player didn't kill them. Then again, it may not warrant an affinity change.
Addressing the player. The player has successfully convinced a belligerent hotel guest to leave Paradiso peacefully via speech challenge instead of using violence.

Addressing the player. The player has just purchased the the secret Tranquilitea tea recipe from Rowan Masterson for Kumar. The companion may be impressed that the player offered their own money to get it. The player could have already failed at trying to negotiate for the tea recipe. They could have also chosen to pickpocket the recipe, but didn't. Maybe it's not right for the player to have agreed to help Kumar steal company secrets.

Addressing the player. The player has just convinced Rowan Masterson to give up a secret Tranquilitea tea recipe via speech challenge. The player could have stolen the tea recipe instead. Maybe the player shouldn't have been helping Kumar get rival company secrets in the first place, though?

The player has used their Security skill to fix a computer issue for Akachi Blum, and thus bypassed the need to wait a day for a technician to fix it.

This is a tricky one. Generally, this area is the worker housing/slums of Paradiso. It's off to the side, off the main drag, so as not to be seen. The housing is kind of ramshackle. It represents the shadier side of Paradiso, outside of the glitz and glamour. Many of the Constant people are more or less happy about this, but some may not like being forced to pay off a debt they never asked for.

The player has been doing a ton of Ranger jobs. The Senior Ranger asks why. Here the player says they did it to further their career.

The Striker gang is offered a chance to go legit and given a real job. It's a little shady, but undoubtedly better than what they were doing. Here the player urges them to take the job.
The player has been caught gang tagging by a corrupt Security Guard. In this option, the player refuses to back down (which will probably mean combat).

After getting caught by Benjamin Bayu, the player is still willing to create drugs for Yannick and Bayu as long as they operate smarter.
After getting caught by Benjamin Bayu, the player is done making drugs for Yannick and now Bayu.
After getting caught by Benjamin Bayu, the player is still game to create more drugs for Yannick (and now Benjamin Bayu).

The player just arranged for a smuggler to have the equivalent of a fake ID and papers to freely smuggle things into Akila City. This is to ostensibly help her poor family who really, really need it, though.
A guard has put people in danger to help out her boss and agitated the dangerous Ashta in Akila. The player says they will cover up her mistake.
A guard has put people in danger to help out her boss and agitated the dangerous Ashta in Akila. The player says they will turn her in (and she'll be fired probably).
A guard has put people in danger to help out her boss and agitated the dangerous Ashta in Akila. The player says they won't do anything if they are bribed.

The player approaches a seedy dive bar in the wrong side of town: Aggies. Clearly it's a place you watch your wallet and your back. People that know Akila City know it's one of the most dangerous places in the city.
There is a Coe Heritage Museum - so a local little museum. It's clear it's a labor of love, charming in its simple way.
In the middle of the pretty unique looking Akila City, there's a Chunks. It's like running into a McDonald's next to Notre Dame.
The player expresses being unwilling to commit crimes to help the scientist gather data (to hopefully help Akila City in the long run)
The player seems willing to commit some serious mischief to help a scientist test something that could help Akila City out.
The player is near the exit to Akila City which goes into Ashta country. So this is where it can get dangerous,
This is the ruins of one of the early structures of Akila City. Preserved for history.
This is the firearms manufacturer Laredo's factory in Akila City. One of the big businesses of the city.
In Akila City there is a statue of the formidable founder of the city (and arguably the whole Freestart Collective): Solomon Coe. One of the most famous people of his age.
In Akila City there is a really chintzy roadside zoo. Think Tiger King.

The player has been asked to deal with squatters in a section of the Well in New Atlantis. These squatters have already put one acquaintance in the hospital, and are occupying a space that a questgiver needs to expand her business. You've reached a junction in the Clinic where hallways branch off into the Research Wing and the High Security wing, both of which have restricted access and guards posted. It's an awful lot of security for a hospital...

You're in a hallway near the entrance of The Clinic, a star station that serves as a hospital in space. It specializes in astrovirology - strange, unique viruses found by people exploring new planets.

You've just walked into the primary patient treatment area of the Clinic. It's high-tech, well maintained, and impressive. Patients with unusual alien viruses or hard to diagnose illnesses are here - not the sort of place you would want to spend time if you're concerned about catching something.
The player has just agreed to smuggle an unknown item from HopeTown to Akila City from an extremely shady character named Mr. Sood. Who knows what's being transported.

The companion has entered the main hall of the Retreat, carved into the side of an actual mountain, with a massive rock column featured prominently in the center of the room, while the floor of the room is raised over a small cave lake. The space is far from being finished as a livable space, but it's pretty impressive for such a remote facility.

The companion has just crossed an idyllic bridge over a nice lake, while standing outside a cozy retreat building in a very remote part of space. It's pretty nice, if a little incongruous for how distant a world this is.
Player describes background of independent living, getting in touch with nature saying: 'It's a beautiful life. Clean, serene, prefect. Get's you in touch with nature and your own soul. '
Player talks about their past and independent living saying about it: 'No hand outs. No taxes. No one but your own conscience telling you what to do. Life on your terms.'
Player describes background of living a rugged independent life, saying: 'It's hard. But rewarding. You're beaten down until everything but the real you is stripped away.'
The player and companion are headed into the Red Mile Course. This course has life or death stakes. They're about to take the elevator down.

The player and companion are near Donovan Rhys, who is sitting at a nearby table with a couple other people. Donovan Rhys is a record-holding Red Mile runner. He's won 28 runs (Most people die in their first run), and has the record for 3rd fastest run in history. If someone's into the Red Mile, they likely know who he is. Donovan is retired now, due to health concerns. Donovan's full of himself, but overall, he's really not a bad guy. Though, he also partakes in big game hunting, which some have an issue with.

The player and companion have arrived at the Red Mile establishment on Porrima III. they're just outside, before going into the main interior. This is intended to be a general comment about this settlement. This planet has changed. It used to be a den and safe haven for criminals, now it's a bloodsport arena and entertainment hub.

The player and companion have entered the Gambling area in Red Mile.There are gambling machines here in the cantina. and the area overlooks the Red mile itself. Many of the patrons here also gamble over someone's life (the Red Mile is a blood sport after all).

Walking into the bar area in the Den. Used to be a playground for the  UC military elite, now it's just a sad, run-down joint that is desperately trying to cling to that old glory.

Player chose to bring Kryx's Legacy to the Crimson Fleet. SysDef is defeated, Delgado is wrapping things up and saying that the future of the Crimson Fleet is now secure thanks to the Player. This would already be passed the moment where Affinity happened, so Companion is dealing with it.

This is the Museum in New Homestead. It's inside an old reconditioned research facility. There are old artifacts from Earth (TBD, please see Phil S. for more details if need be, or keep it vague. The items haven't been made yet, but they'll be things like old electronics, sports memorabilia, etc)

The player and companion have landed in New Homestead. This is on Titan, so it's cold, it's windy, it's cloudy. There are shipping containers littered around, and old research stations (which are now preserved for history) visible. there are also massive wind turbines visible on the outskirts. New Homestead has a small above-ground structure that they're about to enter, on the way down into the main underground portion of the settlement.

This is what amounts to the slums of the New Homestead underground interior. Not that the rest of the accommodations here are all that great, but this area is full of cramped shipping containers stacked on top of each other. It's even inside an old storage area.

In the Ice Mines in New Homestead. They just went through the underground structures of New Homestead, and now they've gone through an airlock into a cave system which is just covered in ice, where workers can be seen chipping away at ice. It's a little odd, but not completely uncommon around the Settled Systems, particularly on inhospitable worlds like this. It's cold in here.

The player and their companion have walked into the Brown Horse Tavern in New Homestead. This is the oldest surviving  restaurant/tavern in the Settled Systems, so it has historical significance. It's quaint compared to other restaurants, like Chunks.

The player gave some advice to struggling student, Adrian Brasilia, about saving up some money to live on before finishing her studies (or, if they have the Professor background, the player can advise Adrian to go to school part time). Instead of just giving her credits, the player has offered this advice, and she feels a little better.

The player has donated a bunch of credits to Adrian Brasilia in New Homestead. Adrian has been having trouble balancing her college studies and making enough credits to put food on the table. Adrian is extremely grateful for this.

The player and a  kidnap victim are in the process of confronting their captor. The captor has asked the player to leave and let the captor kill the victim in exchange for a pile of credits. The player has agreed, siding with the captor and allowing the captor to kill the victim.

The player and a  kidnap victim are in the process of confronting their captor. The captor has asked the player to leave and let the captor kill the victim in exchange for a pile of credits. The player has refused, siding with the victim.

The player's talking to a bartender who's lamenting about the yuppies moving into her town and how sooner or later she's going to have to start serving them. The player has said 'May as well benefit from them being here.'

In front of a small restaurant called 'Offworld Eats.' Despite the name, the aroma is delicious - looks like someone running a decent little quick service place in the Underbelly of Neon.
Standing in front of a place called 'Madame Sauvage's Place.' Looks like some sort of a night club and tough gang types are going in and out. Think 'cyber-bar.'

Standing in front of a bank of Sleepcrates. These are similar to Japanese Capsule Hotels, except they're dirty, cold, wet and in the exterior. It's clearly housing for people who can't afford anything else.

Near Xenofresh's chasmbass processing plant. This is basically the place where Aurora is extracted from the fish, and the rest is ground up and used for other things. It probably stinks and its a working-class area - lots of employees walking around.
Great view of the entirety of Neon's Platform including the huge 'shield' covering the center section. View is from the starport - might be first view of the city (great vantage point).

Walking on the bridge from the Starport to the city's access point (elevators) up to the main terminal - there is a huge 'chemical sniffer' checkpoint ahead - looks like an airport x-ray. They're clearly scanning for something. Also, you're walking way above the crashing oceans below.

Standing in front of the high-tech looking Ryujin Tower. This is the only company with its own complete structure in Neon - they are on the cutting edge of tech and its clear this is the latest and most modern building in town.

Player is looking down Ikuchi Market, a quaint shopping 'arcade' between the Starport Terminal and Ryujin Tower. The arcade is decorated with Asian-inspired motifs (via artwork) and is very clean - think one step under Bayu Plaza - and lined with shops.

In the lobby of the magnificent Trade Tower - the wide staircase ahead leads to the Astral Lounge's entrance, and the lobby itself has elevators clearly heading up to the upper floors - some marked 'Private'. There's also a Kore Kinetics Express store in the lobby and plenty of seating.

Player is standing near the front of the Trade Tower. A huge statue of Benjamin Bayu is proudly displayed in front of the building, echoing the man's obvious arrogance and ego. Beyond is the entrance to the Tower itself.
Player is near a huge drop-off that looks down from the center of Neon straight into the ocean - hundreds of meters down.
Player has just exited the Starport Terminal and is looking up Bayu Plaza towards the huge Trade Tower covered in megacorp logos and the sign for the Astral Lounge.

Description: The player discreetly asked Oliver how they could go about making the ECS Constant disappear (blow up). The companion likely has an opinion on this, since it's kind of sociopathic for the player to even consider as an option.

The player has just told Oliver Campbell that he is going to help outfit the ECS Constant with a grav drive at their own expense. While this does solve the problem, it's not ideal because the Constant has to give up on their home and go searching endlessly through space for a new one. There is no perfectly ideal solution here, though, given the alternatives: resettling the Colonists on Paradiso but turning them into indentured servants, or blowing up the ship. The companion probably has an opinion about the player's choice. Maybe they think it's generous. Maybe they see the actual problem with it, or they might just say 'Well, at least you're not blowing them up...' (which is still technically an option).

The player has just told Oliver Campbell that he is going to help resettle the ECS Constant colonists to Paradiso, but this is going to effectively turn them into indentured servants, forced to stay until they work off their debts. While this does solve the problem, it's not ideal. The player sees a small habitat, used as a trading post and living space by a group of independent settlers. This outpost is a member of LIST, the League of Independent Settlers. What's remarkable about LIST outposts is that they're build far outside  the established Settled System planets. It's amazing that people choose to live out here on the unprotected frontiers of space. It's also great to have a place to trade, craft, and use a mission board on an otherwise unpopulated planet.These comments should be generic - they should be able to work at any LIST outpost. There may be multiple locations made. All outposts will have the same basic size. And every one would have at least the following elements:- a generic, general store vendor- a mission board- one or more crafting workbenches (though the types may be different)- 2 or 3 colonists.

RL012 is the site of a farm, which has been built on a low gravity planet. Biology researchers have been experimenting with ways to grow produce at much larger sizes than would be possible under 'medium' (Earth-like) gravity. In this event, the companion has encountered an area of the farm with several, super-tall 'vertical farm' structures.These are being used to grow hundreds of small seedlings, of an alien plant. (Image Reference, here: https://qph.fs.quoracdn.net/main-qimg-3f1d12df67ff041a6771e532e5d50bfe) The standout feature of these structures is that they are built much taller than would be possible on a 'medium gravity' planet. This location can only spawn on a low gravity planet, which has let these researchers build much taller than normal. These 'seedling towers' are where all planets at the facility begin life. Several different strains are grown here, in search of candidates with the promise to grow much larger. Once they've outgrown the towers, promising individuals are moved to one of the two large warehouses at the location for further, monitored growth.

In this event, the companion has just entered a large warehouse, and is seeing massive produce being grown in a large plant beds. Some of these specimens are several meters in height - towering over the player. These areas are surrounded by scientific equipment, and the companion is surprised and impressed by just how large the scientists have been able to grow these plants.

RL012 is the site of a farm, which has been built on a low gravity planet. Biology researchers have been experimenting with ways to grow produce at much larger sizes than would be possible under 'medium' (Earth-like) gravity. In this event, the companion has just entered the scientists' main lab. Everyone at the site is dead, but there are still several computers sitting on research stations. The companion laments the deaths of all of these researchers (if that make sense), and wonders aloud if any of their research might be salvaged. (There is a computer in this room, where the player can download a recipe to grow the giant plants they were cultivating here).

RL012 is the site of a farm, which has been built on a low gravity planet. Biology researchers have been experimenting with ways to grow produce at much larger sizes than would be possible under 'medium' (Earth-like) gravity. In this event, the companion has just entered the sleeping area for the research team. The room is littered with dead bodies - no one way able to escape the creatures who overran the farm.

RL032 is an Ecliptic military outpost, which is still under construction. During this event, the companion has just entered the shell of a room that's still being built. A large gap in the floor reveals a secret, underground passage below the building - a natural cave-like tunnel through what looks like a solid rock formation from the surface. This passage was not visible from the outside. The companion will be surprised to see it, and they might wonder where it leads.

RL032 is an Ecliptic military outpost, which is still under construction. During this event, the companion has just noticed the remains of a large mudslide, which has collapsed a giant section of concrete foundation that was being built. This collapse has caused the foundation to angle down into a convenient ramp that the player can use to access a higher area (where more enemies are likely present). All companions should comment, with surprise, about discovering the landslide. Depending on their personality, the companion might comment about proceeding with caution. Or - they might instead comment about how the collapse creates a convenient path forward for you both.

RL032 is an Ecliptic military outpost, which is still under construction. During this event, the companion is observing a cargo ship, which the Ecliptic have just used to deliver supplies to the site. Those supplies were in the process of being unloaded when the player arrived. I imagine that most companions would comment about how the Ecliptic must have just gotten a delivery - and wonder if there's anything useful among the supplies for the two of you.

RL032 is an Ecliptic military outpost, which is still under construction. During this event, the companion is standing on a balcony, with a great view of the rest of the location (this will likely be right after you've beat this location's boss). They should comment on the nice view, and specifically call out that they can see your ship from here (doesn't have to be that exact wording - they should just comment, in some way, that it's visible).

RL039 is an oil rig on an alien planet. The rig was overrun by deep sea alien creatures, which swarmed the platform and left no survivors. During this event, the companion has just entered one of the first rooms of the platform. They can see dead bodies lying about. This comment is meant to a 'I've got a bad feeling about this' moment. They are reacting to the eeriness of the situation: A bunch of dead bodies - unknown reasons. It's unclear at this point if anyone is alive on the platform (spoiler: no one is).

RL039 is an oil rig on an alien planet. The rig was overrun by deep sea alien creatures, which swarmed the platform and left no survivors. During this event, the companion walks over to the site of a 'last stand' made by about 6 members of the platform's staff. They had attempted to pile up crates to barricade themselves by some shipping containers. But the creatures swarmed them anyway. Human corpses are strewn about. This comment will likely play right after the player has killed a batch of the creatures who are still loitering at this spot. The companion might wonder out loud about what the hell happened here (on the platform, in general). Where did all of these creatures come from? How did they get onto the platform?

RL039 is an oil rig on an alien planet. The rig was overrun by deep sea alien creatures, which swarmed the platform and left no survivors. During this event, the companion is standing on one of the platform's main, exterior decks. They look up, and notice a window into a large control room. They wonder outloud, about how to get inside and reach it.

RL039 is an oil rig on an alien planet. The rig was overrun by deep sea alien creatures, which swarmed the platform and left no survivors. During this event, the companion has just entered the bedroom/living quarters of the platform crew. Bodies are strewn about. The companion remarks, in mild horror, at how everyone on the platform is dead. No one escaped the swarm of creatures.

The player and their companion stumble upon a mysterious house, built on a remote, tropical planet. As you approach the house, your companion wonders aloud, why someone built this in the middle of nowhere.

The player and their companion have just killed the Terrormorph that wiped out a settlement on Tau Ceti. There are a variety of methods by which this could've happened, but anyone killing a Terrormorph on their own is a big deal.

The player asked Lorelei about her husband Mabhuti because she made it sound like he will die soon. She explains that the eldest members of the ECS Constant crew were expected to voluntarily terminate themselves to make room on the ship due to limited resources and space. Here, the companion should press Lorelei for more information. Some variation on 'What does that mean, exactly?' will play best here, since Lorelei's followup starts with 'It means that...'

The player and companion have just arrived on the ECS Constant, an ancient colony ship. Neither the player. nor the colonists knew what to expect when the player boarded. When greeted at the entrance, the ship's captain seems surprised that the player is human, since they didn't expect to find anyone out here (the ECS Constant left before Earth's collapse and assumed they'd be the first and/or only humans to be here when they arrived) The player responds 'I take it you were expecting something else.'

Frankie is warning the player to NOT go on the third floor. If they do, the Seokga will consider it trespassing and open fire. The player says they'll do whatever they want. Could see it as not caring if they have to kill people or just defying authority.

Player discovered Sean and Dr. Lane were performing illegal human trials, but were also led to believe that they'd get approval any day from Infinity LTD. They are hoping the player will not expose them, but instead the player says, Tough. Enjoy prison.

Player discovered Sean and Dr. Lane were performing illegal human trials, but were also led to believe that they'd get approval any day from Infinity LTD. They are hoping the player will not expose them, but instead the player says, Corporations can't be trusted. Congrats on learning the hard way.

Radiation (like FO4) or gamma rays, or solar flare, or 'space magic.') This is the handwavey-ist damage type. 'Yikes! That can't be good.' (Conditions Target = Ref Casting the Env Dmg spell)

Player's watch is warning them they are about to / are, taking damage from a Corrosive Hazard source. (Something could eat away at the players spacesuit or skin. Acid rain, alien spittle, corrosive pool or gas) Idea here is it could cause nasty chemical burns, eat away at space suit, etc.(Conditions Target = Ref Casting the Env Dmg spell)

Player's watch is warning them they are about to / are, taking damage from an Airborne Hazard source. (Could be toxic gas, alien spore clouds, dust storm, etc. the idea is that it would be a bad idea to breath that in.) (Conditions Target = Ref Casting the Env Dmg spell)
Player's watch is warning them they are about to / are, taking damage from a Thermal Hazard source. (Could be extremely hot OR extremely cold) (Conditions Target = Ref Casting the Env Dmg spell)
Affinity Event for player actions, situations.

Player decided to honor his deal with Rokov by at least cutting him in for some of the pay from the job, evem though Player didn't recommend he join the Crimson Fleet. It was honorable for the Player to not welch on that part of the deal.

DR019 is a spacer star station (a kind of pirate headquarters). In this event, your companion has just followed you into a sky-bridge: a long hallway, with views out into space. At one point, they notice that they can see your ship docked with the station. They should comment - something like 'Hey, I can see your ship from here!' Or similar.

The Player is confronting Gabriel Vera, one of the three people involved in the GalBank conspiracy to defraud it of credits. Unlike Claudia Swist, Vera is standing his ground saying the Player is 'dead wrong' in complete denial of what Swist said about him.
The Player has made a choice here of who to 'throw under the bus' when speaking to Benjamin Bayu about who is profiting from the virus. Player lies and takes the blame.

Affinity Event for use in dialogue responses to respond to something the player says.
The player convinced Natan to reconcile with Rivkah. This is the best, most diplomatic, empathetic ending.

Addressing Jiro. The player has returned to Jiro after helping him with a belligerent hotel guest. In this case, the player has subdued the guest through violence. There are two potential paths that led the player here. In this case, the player attacked first without trying a more diplomatic approach first. The companion might be upset with the player's approach especially since Jiro told them not to make a scene, but they might appreciate that at least the player didn't kill the guest.
The player failed to convince Natan of anything, and Natan is now calling Security. The companion may be impressed that the player is sticking to their promise with Rivkah.
The player agreed to tell Natan where Rivkah is. This is the opposite of what Rivkah wanted, and it will be bad for her.

The player tried to convince Natan, but failed. Natan pressured the player to divulge Rivkah's location, and the player decided to take things into their own hands and attack Natan. Combat is about to start, so keep this response quick.
The player convinced Natan to leave Rivkah be via diplomacy, but then threatened Natan, just to make sure.
The player intimidated Natan into leaving Rivkah alone, then left him with the same threat as choice 5.

The companion notices that there should be an elevator shaft that could be used to gain access to the buried NASA facility below the launch tower.
The companion notices that the lift that allows access into the buried NASA facility lacks power, but it could be used if they could find a way to restore power to it.

The companion can see a large derelict colony ship, unlaunched, abandoned on earth. This isn't their first glimpse of the ship, but they are part way up the launch tower that the ship is connected to.
They are at the top of the derelict launch tower on Earth, they can see very far in every direction.

They are inside a room high up on the abandoned launch tower on earth that would have been used to load people or cargo onto the colony ship, it is covered in sand and debris. There are broken gangways that reach out towards the derelict deteriorating colony ship. There are signs that ship was going to be used, that preparations were being made, but the ship was never loaded and it was abandoned, it isn't clear why.

They enter a room, under the derelict launch tower, long ago buried. The room is badly damaged and covered in debris. It was where astronauts prepared (last minute tests, doning suits, etc.)  before boarding the colony ships. It has medical equipment and is the site of efforts to leave earth. This is the first room they arrive in after using the elevator from the tower.
They drop down through broken floors into a section of rocky ground, a tunnel created by a cave in. It goes deeper underground from the previous room.

They enter into a NASA museum, there are artifacts from past space exploration missions in a large hall. The entire museum is underground and badly damaged. At the far end of the hall it looks like there might be a way to go deeper into the facility.
Through a window in this damaged underground facility a lab or testing chamber of some kind is set up. It looks like this was an area for testing early habitats for use in planetary exploration.
A badly damaged lab with a large cave in. It looks like it might have been a geology lab.
An early vasco/model A prototype is visible in some sort of robotic testing facility chamber.
A funicular leads deeper underground. By this point they are clearly very far underground, inside part of a much larger facility hidden below the launcher tower or the public facing museum.

DR019 is a spacer star station (a kind of pirate headquarters). In this event, your companion has just walked into a storage room of the station, and laid eyes on a large GalBank safe that the pirates have stolen. They call out the presence of the safe, and wonder if the pirates have found a way inside.Note: The pirates have found a way in, and the captain keeps a key to the safe on his person.

DR019 is a spacer star station (a kind of pirate headquarters). In this event, your companion has just followed the player into one of several utility shafts built into the station. These let you slip past rooms of enemies, unnoticed. The companion might remark on how useful or convenient the utility shaft is (kind of a 'don't mind if I do!' moment). There are three utility shafts in the station, but this line will only play the first time your companion enters one.

LC164 is a private mineral mine, which has recently been attacked and taken over by Ecliptic. In this event, the companion is standing in a lab room, looking at a table where the original miners were analyzing some mineral samples.

LC164 is a private mineral mine, which has recently been attacked and taken over by Ecliptic. In this event, the companion is standing in military outpost pod, looking at the bunk beds and living quarters of the original mining crew. The accommodations are rugged, but not low quality. The room illustrates what the life of miners is like in Starfield.

DR029 is a remote landing platform, where pirates are stripping captured ships for parts. In this event, the companion is looking at a locked storage trailer. They wonder aloud, that someone here must know how to get inside.

LC164 is a private mineral mine, which has recently been attacked and taken over by Ecliptic. In this event, the companion is standing in the presence of a massive boring machine. This machine was used by the miners to dig into the ground (creating the cavern where they are standing now). This is the largest piece of mining equipment the companion has ever seen (if that makes sense for the character).

LC164 is a private mineral mine, which has recently been attacked and taken over by Ecliptic. In this event, the companion is standing in a pit, where the Ecliptic tossed the bodies of the original mining team when they took over. The companion is appalled (if that makes sense for the character) that the Ecliptic just tossed the bodies here.

DR029 is a remote landing platform, where pirates are stripping captured ships for parts. In this event, the companion is standing in the center of the platform, on top of the ship the pirates are currently disassembling. They comment about how they feel pretty exposed up there (there aren't many places to hide on top of the ship).

LC122 is an derelict UC refueling star station. It was abandoned by the UC many years ago, and has since been ransacked and looted. In this event, the companion has just boarded the station with the player. Furniture and equipment is tossed everywhere. The companion comments about how this place looks like it's been picked clean by looters.

Companion addresses Jules. The player has just completed Market Research for the ship project. They've completed both types of missions, which will make the ship more ridiculous and versatile. Another opportunity to weigh in on the direction of the design.

Player is speaking to BorealUS (a DJ) in Neon, who is lamenting about all the crowds she has to deal with that demand autographs and dont appreciate her music. Player responds there's no need to get upset.

Player is speaking to BorealUS (a DJ) in Neon, who is lamenting about all the crowds she has to deal with that demand autographs and don't appreciate her music. Player responds that it's the price of stardom. (not very sympathetic)

Player is speaking to BorealUS (a DJ) in Neon, who is lamenting about all the crowds she has to deal with that demand autographs and don't appreciate her music. Player responds that she should be flattered that she has so many fans (trying to make her feel better).

Player is speaking to a shopkeeper who is lamenting that they're overhead is so high due to their shop location and it's financially distressing them. Player responds that 'choice business locations aren't cheap' (trying not to judge)

Player is speaking to a shopkeeper who is lamenting that they're overhead is so high due to their shop location and it's financially distressing them. Player scolds the shopkeeper saying they weren't doing proper financial planning.

Player is speaking to a shopkeeper who is lamenting that they're overhead is so high due to their shop location and it's financially distressing them. Player responds that he has a nice shop and he should be making a fortune (trying to be complimentary).

Player is in Madame Sauvage's Place (a bar) on Neon, and the owner has just stated the 'rules' of the establishment. The Player responds that they completely understood the rules, and won't be any trouble.
Player is in Madame Sauvage's Place (a bar) on Neon, and the owner has just stated the 'rules' of the establishment. The Player responds that they've never been quoted rules at a bar before.
Player is in Madame Sauvage's Place (a bar) on Neon, and the owner has just stated the 'rules' of the establishment. The Player responds that they don't like being told what to do.

Player is speaking to the tough owner of a weapon's shop who has a Model A for protection. He refers to the Model A as his 'complaint department' and the Player responds they won't have any complaints and they're here to spend some credits.

Player is speaking to the tough owner of a weapon's shop who has a Model A for protection. He refers to the Model A as his 'complaint department' and the Player responds that a robot can't handle complaints (just an observation).

Player is speaking to the tough owner of a weapon's shop who has a Model A for protection. He refers to the Model A as his 'complaint department' and the Player responds they prefer dealing with humans and don't like speaking to emotionless robots.

Player is speaking to a shopkeeper on Neon who claimed that they're closed, there's something bothering her and she doesn't want to talk about it. Player uses the Empath trait to sympathize which shortcuts into her spilling the beans (otherwise Player comes back the next day if they want).

Player is speaking to a shopkeeper on Neon who claimed that they're closed, there's something bothering her and she doesn't want to talk about it. Player uses the Leadership trait to sympathize which shortcuts into her spilling the beans (otherwise Player comes back the next day if they want).

Player is speaking to the Trade Authority vendor in Neon. They clearly allude that they'll deal in stolen goods. Player responds by saying that buying and selling stolen goods is illegal.
Player is speaking to the Trade Authority vendor in Neon. They clearly allude that they'll deal in stolen goods. Player responds by saying that 'you don't seem to be afraid of getting caught.'

Player is speaking to the Trade Authority vendor in Neon. They clearly allude that they'll deal in stolen goods. Player responds that stolen goods are their specific area of interest (saying they dont mind crime).

Player is speaking to the co-owner of a food place in Neon. He's been complaining about the corruption that runs rampant in the city saying that Bayu makes it hard for honest people to earn a living. The Player says something should be done about Bayu (in heavy agreement).

Player is speaking to the co-owner of a food place in Neon. He's been complaining about the corruption that runs rampant in the city saying that Bayu makes it hard for honest people to earn a living. The Player suggests he gets the Freestar Rangers involved.

Player is speaking to the co-owner of a food place in Neon. He's been complaining about the corruption that runs rampant in the city saying that Bayu makes it hard for honest people to earn a living. The Player can say that 'maybe honest people shouldn't work here' (which is rude)

Player is speaking to the person in charge of security at the Neon Starport entry who explains that the transport of Aurora is illegal (it has to stay in the city). It's just a warning, explaining the situation, but Player responds that they wouldn't dream of transporting Aurora.

Player is speaking to the person in charge of security at the Neon Starport entry who explains that the transport of Aurora is illegal (it has to stay in the city). It's just a warning, explaining the situation, but Player responds that only use it for medicinal purposes, being flippant. Companion may or may not be amused.

LC039 is the Generdyne power plant interior, in Neon. The player is intruding (and possibly sneaking) through the plant. In this event, the player and the companion have just broken into a restricted side entrance to the plant. They find themselves in a small room, and walk over to windows that look out into a large research lab room. The room is packed with engineers and security guards. The security guards are (figuratively) breathing down the necks of the engineers - watching them very closely. It doesn't look like an enjoyable work situation for the engineers.

LC039 is the Generdyne power plant interior, in Neon. The player is intruding (and possibly sneaking) through the plant. In this event, the player and the companion have just snuck into a room with some large, scientific machinery. This equipment is positioned in the center of the room, and sticks up into the floor above, through a hole in the ceiling. The companion notices this hole in the ceiling.

LC039 is the Generdyne power plant interior, in Neon. The player is intruding (and possibly sneaking) through the plant. In this event, the player and the companion have just entered a large utility hallway, which runs alongside some massive electric conduit cables. The cables are absolutely huge, and the companion might wonder where they lead, or what purpose they serve.

LC039 is the Generdyne power plant interior, in Neon. The player is intruding (and possibly sneaking) through the plant. In this event, the companion has followed the player into an air vent, which travels above a large, electrical control room. The companion has walked over to a vent in the floor of the shaft, from which they can see down in the room. It's filled with people (engineers and security guards) - people that the companion is glad to be avoiding.

LC039 is the Generdyne power plant interior, in Neon. The player is intruding (and possibly sneaking) through the plant. In this event, the companion has just entered the 'power core room' - a large, refrigerated chamber, containing 6, story-tall batteries. 6 giant conduit cables (like the one you both encountered earlier) feed into the room from all directions, and each one connects to one of the large batteries. There are a number of reactions a companion could have to seeing this room: They might just be impressed with the scale of the room. They might be really cold, and notice that the room seems to have chilled gas pumped into it to keep the batteries cool. They might notice that this seems to be the termination point of all conduit cables in the power plant (answering the question posed in an earlier comment). They might also understand that this room is responsible for powering the entirety of Neon, and may be one of the oldest places in Neon.

LC039 is the Generdyne power plant interior, in Neon. The player is intruding (and possibly sneaking) through the plant. In this event, the player and the companion are looking out of a window in a Generdyne Executive's office. This office window looks down onto a lab, where engineers are working on a research project. The companion might comment that it must be stressful to be constantly watched by the boss, while you work.
